rationale: The synthetic determinism check is not provable in this Verus version. The conclusion `post1_self_ == post2_self_` is structural equality on `StrictlyOrderedMap<K>`, which contains `Vec<K>` (inside `keys`) and `Vec<ID>` exec fields. We verified that `Vec<T>` in this Verus version lacks view-based extensionality (a small test `requires v1@ == v2@ ensures v1 == v2` fails), and the same gap exists for `=~=` (a test `requires v1@ == v2@ ensures v1 =~= v2` fails). The `Ghost<Map<K,ID>>` field IS view-extensional, but that alone cannot close struct equality. The hypotheses (`valid()`, view == `pre@.insert(k,v)`, identical gap structure) pin down the views of all fields, but Verus has no axiom to lift Seq-level view equality to abstract `Vec` SMT equality, and we are forbidden from introducing `assume`/`external_body`/`assume_specification` to add such an axiom. Hence the implication `H ==> post1_self_ == post2_self_` is not derivable here. Tried: (1) direct `assert(post1_self_ == post2_self_
